__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source that scrapes the LLM Gateway

panels.description: How many LLM calls are being routed to each provider (rate over 5m).
panels.title: LLM Calls per Provider (rate)

panels.description: Share of total LLM calls handled by each provider.
panels.title: Call Distribution by Provider

panels.description: Success vs error outcomes per provider.
panels.title: Outcomes per Provider (success/error)

panels.description: Total calls handled by each provider since startup.
panels.title: Total Calls per Provider

panels.description: REST API turnaround time per endpoint (p95) from the built-in http_server_requests timer. This is the end-to-end time each gateway REST endpoint takes to respond.
panels.title: REST API Turnaround — p95 per Endpoint

panels.description: Average REST API turnaround time per endpoint = rate(sum)/rate(count). Works even without histogram buckets.

targets.expr: sum by (uri) (rate(http_server_requests_seconds_sum{application="$application", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum by (uri) (rate(http_server_requests_seconds_count{application="$application", uri!~"/actuator.*"}[5m])), 0.0001)

panels.title: REST API Turnaround — Avg per Endpoint

panels.description: Gateway operation turnaround (@Timed llm.gateway.execution) split by operation: execute / failover / auto-failover.
panels.title: Gateway Execution Turnaround (@Timed)

panels.description: Per-provider LLM call latency (p50/p95/p99) from llm_request_latency_seconds.
panels.title: LLM Provider Call Latency (p95)

panels.description: Token consumption rate per provider (prompt + completion).
panels.title: Token Usage Rate per Provider

panels.description: Cumulative tokens by type (prompt vs completion).
panels.title: Tokens by Provider & Type

panels.description: Errors by type per provider.
panels.title: Errors by Type (rate)

panels.description: Resilience4j circuit-breaker state (0=closed, 1=open, 2=half-open).
panels.title: Circuit Breaker State

panels.description: The uptime of the Java virtual machine.
panels.title: Uptime

panels.description: Process start time since unix epoch — effectively the last restart time of the gateway.
panels.title: Last Restart Time

panels.description: Number of processors available to the JVM.
panels.title: CPU Count

panels.description: Percentage of max heap currently used.
panels.title: Heap Used %

panels.description: Percentage of max non-heap currently used.
panels.title: Non-Heap Used %

panels.description: JVM memory used split by area (heap vs nonheap).
panels.title: JVM Memory Used (heap / nonheap)

panels.description: Heap memory used per pool (eden / survivor / old gen).
panels.title: Heap Used by Pool

panels.description: Non-heap memory used per pool (metaspace / code cache / compressed class space).
panels.title: Non-Heap Used by Pool

panels.description: System vs JVM-process CPU usage.
panels.title: CPU Usage

panels.description: System load average over the last minute.
panels.title: Load Average

panels.description: Live, daemon and peak thread counts.
panels.title: Threads (live / daemon / peak)

panels.description: Number of threads in each JVM thread state.
panels.title: Thread States

panels.description: Garbage collection rate (collections/sec) by GC action and cause.
panels.title: GC Count (rate)

panels.description: Time spent in GC pauses by action and cause.
panels.title: GC Pause Duration

panels.description: Classes currently loaded and total classes unloaded since startup.
panels.title: Classes Loaded / Unloaded

panels.description: Committed JVM memory by area — memory guaranteed available to the JVM.
panels.title: Committed Memory by Area

panels.description: GC allocation rate (young gen) and promotion rate (to old gen).
panels.title: GC Allocated / Promoted (rate)

panels.description: Application log events per second by level (from logback_events_total). Errors and warnings are highlighted.
panels.title: Log Events (rate by level)

panels.description: Open file descriptors vs the OS maximum for the JVM process.
panels.title: File Descriptors (open / max)

panels.description: JVM buffer pools — direct and mapped buffer memory used vs total capacity.
panels.title: Buffer Pools (direct / mapped)

panels.description: Process native memory from micrometer-jvm-extras: RSS (resident), VSS (virtual), PSS, and swap. Linux-only — populated in the Docker runtime, no-op on macOS/Windows.
panels.title: Process Memory (RSS / VSS / PSS / Swap)
